title: The Old Stone Bridge
summary: A narrator walks across an old stone bridge in a quiet English town and describes the beautiful sights and sounds all around him from the middle of the bridge.
body: I am standing on an old stone bridge in the middle of a small English town. The bridge is wide enough for two people to walk side by side. The stones under my feet are smooth and worn from many years of use. I look down at the river below. The water is clear and slow. I can see small fish moving just below the surface. On one side of the bridge, there is a row of old houses with red brick walls and small white windows. Flower boxes hang below the windows and they are full of yellow and pink flowers. On the other side, I can see a grassy park with tall oak trees. Two ducks swim quietly in the shallow water near the bank. The town is very peaceful. I can hear birds singing somewhere above me and the gentle sound of the water moving under the bridge. A woman walks past me with a small dog. She nods and smiles but does not speak. I stand here for a long time and just listen.

annotations.exampleEn: They crossed the river using an old stone bridge.
The steps were worn smooth by thousands of feet over the years.
A leaf floated on the surface of the water.
Many old English houses are built with red brick walls.

annotations.meaningVi: hộp trồng hoa (gắn ngoài cửa sổ)
annotations.exampleEn: The window ledge had a flower box full of red geraniums.
The children played on the grassy hill near the school.
A squirrel climbed the tall oak tree in the park.
The children paddled in the shallow part of the river.
We sat on the bank of the river and watched the boats.
The countryside is so peaceful compared to the city.
A gentle breeze blew through the open window.
He nodded to say hello as she passed by.

grammarNotes.title: Present Simple for Descriptions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Mặc dù bài này kể về một khoảnh khắc cụ thể, nó dùng thì hiện tại đơn (present simple) để mô tả cảnh vật như thể nó đang diễn ra ngay trước mắt bạn: 'I am standing...', 'The water is clear...', 'Two ducks swim quietly...' Cách này tạo ra cảm giác bạn đang ở ngay đó cùng người kể chuyện.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: This passage uses the present simple and present continuous to create a vivid, immediate description. 'I am standing on the bridge' (present continuous = happening right now). 'The water is clear and slow' / 'Two ducks swim quietly' (present simple = describing what is always true or visible in the scene). This makes descriptions feel alive.

grammarNotes.title: Prepositions of Place: on, below, above, near, under
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Bài này dùng nhiều giới từ chỉ vị trí để mô tả phong cảnh: 'on the bridge' (trên cầu), 'below the surface' (dưới bề mặt), 'above me' (phía trên tôi), 'near the bank' (gần bờ), 'under the bridge' (dưới gầm cầu). Những giới từ này rất quan trọng để mô tả vị trí trong tiếng Anh.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: This passage uses many place prepositions to build the scene: 'on the bridge', 'below the surface', 'above me', 'near the bank', 'under the bridge'. Learning these prepositions helps you describe where things are in relation to each other — an essential skill for descriptions.

translationVi: Tôi đang đứng trên một cây cầu đá cũ ở giữa một thị trấn nhỏ của nước Anh. Cây cầu đủ rộng để hai người đi sóng vai nhau. Những viên đá dưới chân tôi trơn nhẵn và mòn cũ qua nhiều năm sử dụng. Tôi nhìn xuống con sông phía dưới. Nước trong vắt và chảy chậm. Tôi có thể thấy những con cá nhỏ đang bơi ngay dưới bề mặt nước. Ở một bên cầu, có một dãy nhà cũ với tường gạch đỏ và những cửa sổ nhỏ màu trắng. Những hộp hoa treo phía dưới cửa sổ và đầy ắp hoa vàng và hoa hồng. Ở phía bên kia, tôi có thể nhìn thấy một công viên xanh mướt với những cây sồi cao lớn. Hai con vịt đang bơi nhẹ nhàng ở vùng nước nông gần bờ. Thị trấn rất yên bình. Tôi có thể nghe tiếng chim hót đâu đó phía trên và tiếng nước chảy nhẹ nhàng dưới gầm cầu. Một người phụ nữ đi ngang qua tôi với một chú chó nhỏ. Bà gật đầu và mỉm cười nhưng không nói gì. Tôi đứng đây một lúc lâu và chỉ lắng nghe.

options: At the entrance of a market
By the side of the river

questions.explanation: The story says 'The stones under my feet are smooth and worn from many years of use.'

questions.explanation: The story says 'I can see small fish moving just below the surface.'
questions.hint: What can be seen just under the water?

questions.explanation: The story says 'a row of old houses with red brick walls.'
questions.hint: What material are the house walls made from?

questions.prompt: What colours are the flowers in the window boxes?
questions.explanation: The story says 'they are full of yellow and pink flowers.'

options: A grassy park with tall oak trees

questions.hint: What is on the opposite side from the houses?

options: In the shallow water near the bank
In the middle of the river
questions.explanation: The story says 'Two ducks swim quietly in the shallow water near the bank.'
questions.hint: Where exactly do the ducks swim?

questions.prompt: What sounds does the narrator hear?
options: Birds singing and the gentle sound of water
questions.explanation: The story says 'I can hear birds singing somewhere above me and the gentle sound of the water moving under the bridge.'
questions.hint: What two natural sounds are mentioned?

questions.prompt: What does the woman with the dog do?
options: She stops and talks to the narrator
She nods and smiles but does not speak
She waves and walks quickly past
questions.explanation: The story says 'She nods and smiles but does not speak.'
questions.hint: How does the woman acknowledge the narrator without stopping?

questions.prompt: What does the narrator do at the very end?
options: He walks back to the town
He sits down on the bridge
questions.explanation: The story ends with 'I stand here for a long time and just listen.'
questions.hint: What does the narrator decide to do with his senses?

cloze.template: I am standing on an old stone bridge in the middle of a small English town. The bridge is wide enough for two people to walk side by side. The stones under my feet are smooth and {{b1}} from many years of use. I look down at the river below. The water is clear and slow. I can see small fish moving just below the {{b2}}. On one side of the bridge, there is a row of old houses with red {{b3}} walls and small white windows. Flower boxes hang below the windows and they are full of yellow and pink flowers. On the other side, I can see a grassy park with tall {{b4}} trees. Two ducks swim {{b5}} in the shallow water near the bank. The town is very {{b6}}. I can hear birds singing somewhere above me and the {{b7}} sound of the water moving under the bridge. A woman walks past me with a small {{b8}}. She {{b9}} and smiles but does not speak. I stand here for a long time and just {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: 'Worn' describes something made smooth or thin through long use — showing the bridge's age.
The 'surface' is the top layer of the water — the fish are visible just below it.
'Red brick' is a classic building material used in many traditional English houses.
'Oak trees' are large, strong trees common in English parks and countryside.
'Swim quietly' paints a calm, peaceful scene — the ducks are not disturbing anything.
'Peaceful' means calm and quiet — this town has no rush or noise.
'Gentle sound' means a soft, calm sound — not harsh or startling.
The woman is described as walking 'with a small dog' — a common sight in English towns.
'Nods' means to move the head down briefly — a quiet, polite greeting between strangers.
The narrator chooses to simply 'listen' — fully taking in the peaceful sounds around him.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator stands on the bridge for a long time and 'just listens' — he does not check his phone or hurry anywhere. In your daily life, do you ever stop and pay attention to the sounds and sights around you? Why might this be good for a person's mind?
